findings.evidence: Mathlib has `SemimoduleCat` zero morphisms as a class (`mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Semi.lean:317: instance : HasZeroMorphisms (SemimoduleCat.{v} R)`); this PR has the needed laws at `ComoduleCat.lean:291` and `ComoduleCat.lean:298` but only as standalone simp theorems.
findings.fix: Add `instance : CategoryTheory.Limits.HasZeroMorphisms (ComoduleCat.{u, v, w} R C)` using the existing zero morphisms and the proven composition laws.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat` defines `Zero (M ⟶ N)` and proves `zero_comp`/`comp_zero`, but does not package them as the standard `CategoryTheory.Limits.HasZeroMorphisms` instance, so generic zero-morphism category API will not apply.

summary: The hom additive/module structure is at the right semiring level, but the categorical zero API stops short of the standard Mathlib class despite proving its fields.
